what: Access NOAA Climate and Weather Data
title: Access NOAA Climate and Weather Data
text: readnoaa provides clean, tidy access climate weather data NOAA (National Oceanic Atmospheric Administration) directly R. API key required.

title: Access NOAA Climate and Weather Data
text: National Oceanic Atmospheric Administration US federal agency responsible monitoring weather, oceans, atmosphere. National Centers Environmental Information (NCEI) world’s largest archive weather climate data, hosting observations 100,000 stations across 180 countries, records stretching back 1700s. NCEI maintains Data Service API, provides free, open access archive. Unlike many government data APIs, requires API key - can start pulling data immediately. API returns clean CSV data current approximately 2-3 days ago.

title: Access NOAA Climate and Weather Data
text: NOAA’s archive covers wide range weather climate variables. Daily observations include maximum minimum temperature, precipitation, snowfall, snow depth, wind speed. Monthly annual summaries aggregate averages totals. data spans land-based weather stations, marine buoys, airport observation sites worldwide. Beyond current observations, NOAA publishes 30-year climate normals: statistical baselines calculated 1991-2020 period represent “typical” weather given location. widely used agriculture, energy, construction, climate research understand current conditions compare long-term averages. archive also includes hourly observations, 15-minute precipitation data, local climatological records specialised use cases.

title: Access NOAA Climate and Weather Data
text: flagship R package NOAA data, rnoaa (~3,300 downloads/month peak), archived CRAN February 2024 NOAA deprecated CDO v2 API. planned rOpenSci replacement never materialised. remaining CRAN package (noaa, 3 functions, ~180 downloads/month) still targets broken old API. readnoaa fills gap targeting NOAA’s current NCEI Data Service v1 API. provides dedicated functions common datasets (daily observations, monthly/annual summaries, climate normals) plus generic fetcher full archive. Station discovery functions help find stations location name.

title: Access NOAA Climate and Weather Data
code: install.packages("readnoaa")  # Or install the development version from GitHub # install.packages("devtools") devtools::install_github("charlescoverdale/readnoaa")

title: Access NOAA Climate and Weather Data
text: Every NOAA data request needs station ID. two ways find stations: 1. Search location using noaa_nearby(): 2. Search name bounding box using noaa_stations():
code: # Stations within 50 km of Sydney, Australia noaa_nearby(-33.87, 151.21, radius_km = 50) #>        station                    name latitude longitude distance_km #>   ASN00066062 SYDNEY OBSERVATORY HILL   -33.86    151.21         1.1 #>   ASN00066006 SYDNEY AIRPORT AMO       -33.95    151.17         9.8 #>   ... # Search by name noaa_stations(text = "Heathrow") #>        station              name latitude longitude elevation #>   UKW00035054 HEATHROW            51.478    -0.449      25.3 #>   ...  # Search by bounding box (south, west, north, east) noaa_stations(bbox = c(35, -120, 40, -115)) #>        station                    name latitude longitude elevation #>   USC00040232   BAKERSFIELD MEADOWS FLD   35.433  -119.050     150.0 #>   ...

title: Access NOAA Climate and Weather Data
text: Use list_datatypes() discover available variables given dataset station.

title: Access NOAA Climate and Weather Data
text: NCEI applies automated quality control checks observations, flagging approximately 0.3% values. can include flags setting include_flags = TRUE: adds attribute columns alongside data column containing measurement quality flag codes. Flags useful filtering suspect observations research workflows. include station coordinates (latitude, longitude, elevation) observation, use include_location = TRUE.
code: df <- noaa_daily("USW00094728", "2024-01-01", "2024-01-31",                  datatypes = c("TMAX", "TMIN"), include_flags = TRUE)

title: Access NOAA Climate and Weather Data
text: Data cached locally tools::R_user_dir("readnoaa", "cache") first download. Subsequent calls parameters return instantly cache. force fresh download, pass cache = FALSE. clear entire cache, call clear_cache().

title: Access NOAA Climate and Weather Data
text: Daily observations come Global Historical Climatology Network - Daily (GHCN-Daily), integrates data 100,000 stations across 180 countries. Monthly annual summaries derived Global Summary Month/Year datasets. Climate normals follow WMO guidelines calculating 30-year averages.

title: Access NOAA Climate and Weather Data
text: NOAA data produced US federal government public domain. restrictions use, redistribution, modification. NCEI Data Service API free requires API key, enforce rate limits. package automatically throttles requests retries rate-limit errors. Daily data requests spanning one year automatically split yearly chunks avoid API timeouts. Data typically available 2-3 days behind real time, station coverage varies — stations gaps limited variable availability. package affiliated endorsed NOAA.

title: Daily weather observations — noaa_daily
text: Returns daily weather data NCEI Daily Summaries dataset (GHCN-Daily). Common data types include TMAX (maximum temperature), TMIN (minimum temperature), PRCP (precipitation), SNOW (snowfall), SNWD (snow depth).

title: Daily weather observations — noaa_daily
code: noaa_daily(   station,   start_date,   end_date,   datatypes = NULL,   units = "metric",   include_flags = FALSE,   include_location = FALSE,   cache = TRUE )

title: Daily weather observations — noaa_daily
text: station Character. One station IDs (e.g. "USW00094728" Central Park, NYC). start_date Character. Start date "YYYY-MM-DD" "YYYY-MM" format. end_date Character. End date format. datatypes Optional character vector data type codes retrieve (e.g. c("TMAX", "TMIN")). NULL, available types returned. units Character. "metric" (default, Celsius/mm) "standard" (Fahrenheit/inches). include_flags Logical. Include data quality flags NCEI (default FALSE). include_location Logical. Include station latitude, longitude, elevation columns (default FALSE). cache Logical. Use cached data available (default TRUE).

title: Daily weather observations — noaa_daily
text: data frame columns including: station Character. Station identifier. date Date. Observation date. name Character. Station name. ... Numeric. Data columns vary station request (e.g. tmax, tmin, prcp).

title: Daily weather observations — noaa_daily
text: Requests spanning one year automatically split yearly chunks avoid API timeouts.

what: Find stations near a location — noaa_nearby
title: Find stations near a location — noaa_nearby
text: Searches weather stations within given radius point, sorted distance. Uses GHCN-Daily station inventory.

title: Find stations near a location — noaa_nearby
code: noaa_nearby(lat, lon, radius_km = 50, limit = 25L)

title: Find stations near a location — noaa_nearby
text: lat Numeric. Latitude target location. lon Numeric. Longitude target location. radius_km Numeric. Search radius kilometres (default 50). limit Integer. Maximum number results (default 25).

title: Find stations near a location — noaa_nearby
text: data frame columns noaa_stations() plus: distance_km Numeric. Distance target point kilometres.

title: Find stations near a location — noaa_nearby
code: # \donttest{ op <- options(readnoaa.cache_dir = tempdir()) # Stations within 25 km of central London noaa_nearby(51.5, -0.1, radius_km = 25) #> ℹ Searching for nearby stations #> ✔ Searching for nearby stations [2.4s] #>  #>       station latitude longitude elevation      name distance_km #> 1 UKE00105915  51.5608    0.1789       137 HAMPSTEAD    20.44295 options(op) # }

title: Climate normals (1991-2020) — noaa_normals
text: Returns 30-year climate normals NCEI Normals dataset. Normals represent average climate conditions 1991-2020 period.

title: Climate normals (1991-2020) — noaa_normals
code: noaa_normals(   station,   period = "monthly",   datatypes = NULL,   include_flags = FALSE,   include_location = FALSE,   cache = TRUE )

title: Climate normals (1991-2020) — noaa_normals
text: station Character. One station IDs. period Character. One "monthly", "daily", "annual". datatypes Optional character vector data type codes. include_flags Logical. Include data quality flags NCEI (default FALSE). include_location Logical. Include station latitude, longitude, elevation columns (default FALSE). cache Logical. Use cached data available (default TRUE).

title: Climate normals (1991-2020) — noaa_normals
text: data frame. Columns vary period typically include station, date month, normal values temperature, precipitation, variables.

title: Search for weather stations — noaa_stations
text: Searches GHCN-Daily station inventory bounding box text query. station list (~120,000 stations worldwide) downloaded cached locally.

title: Search for weather stations — noaa_stations
code: noaa_stations(bbox = NULL, text = NULL, limit = 25L, cache = TRUE)

title: Search for weather stations — noaa_stations
text: bbox Optional numeric vector length 4 defining bounding box: c(south_lat, west_lon, north_lat, east_lon). text Optional character string search station names (case-insensitive). limit Integer. Maximum number results (default 25). cache Logical. Use cached station list available (default TRUE).

title: Search for weather stations — noaa_stations
text: data frame columns: station Character. Station identifier. name Character. Station name. latitude Numeric. Latitude decimal degrees. longitude Numeric. Longitude decimal degrees. elevation Numeric. Elevation metres.

title: Search for weather stations — noaa_stations
code: # \donttest{ op <- options(readnoaa.cache_dir = tempdir()) # Search for stations in London area noaa_stations(bbox = c(51.3, -0.5, 51.7, 0.3)) #> ℹ Searching for stations #> ✔ Searching for stations [175ms] #>  #>       station latitude longitude elevation      name #> 1 UKE00105915  51.5608    0.1789     137.0 HAMPSTEAD #> 2 UKM00003772  51.4780   -0.4610      25.3  HEATHROW  # Search by name noaa_stations(text = "Heathrow") #> ℹ Searching for stations #> ✔ Searching for stations [263ms] #>  #>       station latitude longitude elevation     name #> 1 UKE00107650  51.4789    0.4489      25.0 HEATHROW #> 2 UKM00003772  51.4780   -0.4610      25.3 HEATHROW options(op) # }

what: readnoaa: Access 'NOAA' Climate and Weather Data — readnoaa-package
title: readnoaa: Access 'NOAA' Climate and Weather Data — readnoaa-package
text: Provides clean, tidy access climate weather data 'National Oceanic Atmospheric Administration' ('NOAA') via 'National Centers Environmental Information' ('NCEI') Data Service API https://www.ncei.noaa.gov/access/services/data/v1. Covers daily weather observations, monthly annual summaries, 30-year climate normals 100,000 stations across 180 countries. API key required. Dedicated functions handle common datasets, generic fetcher provides access 'NCEI' datasets. Station discovery functions help users find stations location name. Data downloaded first use cached locally subsequent calls. package endorsed certified 'NOAA'.

title: readnoaa: Access 'NOAA' Climate and Weather Data — readnoaa-package
text: Maintainer: Charles Coverdale charlesfcoverdale@gmail.com [copyright holder]

title: readnoaa 0.1.2
text: Fixed edge case CSV numeric coercion columns -NA original values incorrectly converted.

title: readnoaa 0.1.1
text: CRAN release: 2026-03-19 Examples now cache tempdir() instead user’s home directory, fixing CRAN policy compliance \donttest examples. Cache directory now configurable via options(readnoaa.cache_dir = ...).

title: readnoaa 0.1.0
text: Initial release. Daily, monthly, annual, climate normals data retrieval. Generic fetcher NCEI dataset. Station discovery location, bounding box, text search. Local caching downloaded data.
